backlog.title: v0.3.0 Typed-Command Transport
backlog.description: Upgrade the qmk_notifier crate from v0.2.1 (legacy string-send only, replies discarded) to v0.3.0 (typed-command transport + reply parsing). This is the complete delta described in PRD.md §3 and §10: new RunCommand variants (QueryInfo, QueryCallback, SetOs, ApplyHostContext), HostOs enum, CommandResponse enum, typed-command framing [0x81][0x9F][0xF0][cmd][args][0x03], reply parsing (0x51 typed vs 0/1 legacy vs timeout), evolved burst_to_one/send_raw_report to capture replies, run() returns CommandResponse, dependency cleanup, version bump, and documentation sync.

milestones.title: Type Contracts & Framing Constants
milestones.description: Establish the foundational type contracts that all downstream work depends on: HostOs enum, extended RunCommand with typed variants, CommandResponse enum, typed-command wire constants, and the run() signature change to return CommandResponse. After this milestone the crate compiles with all new types in place, run() returns CommandResponse (with placeholder/stub logic for typed commands), and existing tests are updated to match the new return type.

tasks.title: Add typed-command public API types to lib.rs
tasks.description: Add the HostOs enum, extend RunCommand with typed variants, and add the CommandResponse enum. These are the public API surface types defined in PRD §3.

subtasks.title: Add HostOs enum to lib.rs
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_b92a9b2b603f/architecture/firmware_wire_contract.md — the os_variant_t values are 0=UNSURE, 1=LINUX, 2=WINDOWS, 3=MACOS, 4=IOS. See also notifier.h NOTIFY_CMD_SET_OS. The HostOs enum mirrors QMK's os_variant_t exactly.
2. INPUT: No dependencies. This is a new standalone type in src/lib.rs.
3. LOGIC: Add to src/lib.rs (after the existing RunCommand enum definition). Implement:
   ```rust
   /// Host operating system, mirrors QMK's `os_variant_t`.
   /// Sent via SET_OS (cmd 0x03) to declare the host OS at connect.
   #[repr(u8)]
   #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
   pub enum HostOs {
       Unsure = 0,
       Linux = 1,
       Windows = 2,
       Macos = 3,
       Ios = 4,
   }
   ```
   The `#[repr(u8)]` is critical — `as u8` cast produces the wire byte. Add a unit test asserting each discriminant value matches the firmware contract.
4. OUTPUT: A public `HostOs` type in lib.rs, consumable by RunCommand::SetOs variant (next subtask) and build_command_data (P1.M2.T1).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Add JSDoc-style doc comments on the enum and each variant (shown above). No separate docs file needed — this is an internal type addition with no config/API surface change beyond the type itself.

subtasks.title: Extend RunCommand enum with typed-command variants
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_b92a9b2b603f/architecture/firmware_wire_contract.md §Command Table for exact cmd_ids and argument layouts. The RunCommand enum currently has only SendMessage(String) and ListDevices. It is defined in src/lib.rs around line 21.
2. INPUT: Depends on P1.M1.T1.S1 (HostOs enum must exist for SetOs variant).
3. LOGIC: Extend the existing `pub enum RunCommand` in src/lib.rs to add four new variants per PRD §3:
   ```rust
   pub enum RunCommand {
       SendMessage(String),
       ListDevices,
       QueryInfo,                                              // cmd 0x01
       QueryCallback(u8),                                      // cmd 0x02, arg = index
       SetOs(HostOs),                                          // cmd 0x03
       ApplyHostContext { layer: Option<u8>, callbacks: Vec<u8>, clear_board: bool }, // cmd 0x05
   }
   ```
   For ApplyHostContext: `layer = None` means 0xFF (clear host layer); `Some(n)` means host-layer number (>=224 by convention). `callbacks` is the full desired enabled callback-id set. `clear_board = true` means set the firmware clear_board flag bit.
   Add #[derive(Debug, Clone)] to match existing derives.
   IMPORTANT: This will cause a compile error in run() because the match is no longer exhaustive. That is expected — P1.M1.T2.S2 adds the stub arms. If you need the crate to compile before T2, add temporary `todo!()` arms. Add unit tests for the new variants' construction.
4. OUTPUT: Extended RunCommand enum. Consumed by build_command_data (P1.M2.T1) and run() dispatch (P1.M3.T3).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Doc-comment each variant referencing the cmd_id and PRD §10.1. No separate docs file — the public API doc comments are the documentation.

subtasks.title: Add CommandResponse enum to lib.rs

tasks.title: Add framing constants and evolve run() signature
tasks.description: Add typed-command wire constants to core.rs and change run() to return Result<CommandResponse, QmkError>. After this task, the crate compiles with the new return type, existing tests are updated, and typed-command variants have stub arms awaiting real dispatch logic in M3.

subtasks.title: Add typed-command constants to core.rs
Change run() to return CommandResponse and fix existing tests
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_b92a9b2b603f/architecture/findings_and_risks.md §R5 — the test `test_run_with_send_message_command` matches `Ok(())` which WILL break when the Ok type changes from `()` to `CommandResponse`. Also see system_context.md §qmkonnect consumer — qmkonnect uses `Ok(_) => ...` so it remains source-compatible.
2. INPUT: Depends on P1.M1.T1.S3 (CommandResponse must exist). If P1.M1.T1.S2 (RunCommand variants) is done, the match in run() needs stub arms for the new variants.
3. LOGIC: In src/lib.rs:
   a) Change run() signature: `pub fn run(params: RunParameters) -> Result<CommandResponse, QmkError>`
   b) For RunCommand::SendMessage: after successful send_raw_report (still returns Result<(), QmkError> at this stage), return `Ok(CommandResponse::Legacy { matched: true })` as a PLACEHOLDER. Real reply parsing arrives in P1.M3.T3.
   c) For RunCommand::ListDevices: after list_hid_devices(), return `Ok(CommandResponse::Timeout)` (semantic: no device reply was received — nothing was sent).
   d) For typed variants (if they exist from S2): add `todo!()` stub arms — real dispatch is P1.M3.T3.
   e) In src/main.rs: update to handle the new return type. Currently main.rs calls `run(params)` and checks `Err`. With the new return type, main.rs should print the CommandResponse on Ok (e.g., `println!("{}", response)` — this requires CommandResponse to implement Display, OR main.rs can use debug print `println!("{:?}", response)`). Use Debug print for now.
   f) Fix existing tests in lib.rs: `test_run_with_send_message_command` matches `Ok(())` — change to `Ok(CommandResponse::Legacy { matched: true }) => {}` or use `Ok(_) => {}`. Similarly for `test_run_with_verbose_output`.
4. OUTPUT: run() returns Result<CommandResponse, QmkError>. Crate compiles. All tests pass. Placeholder logic for typed commands.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the run() doc comment to describe the new return type and link to PRD §3 and §10. The lib.rs-level doc comment should mention CommandResponse. No separate docs file.

milestones.title: Pure Framing & Reply Parsing Functions
milestones.description: Implement the pure, hardware-free functions that build typed-command payloads from RunCommand variants and parse 32-byte device replies into CommandResponse. These are the heart of the typed-command transport and are fully unit-testable without any HID hardware.

tasks.title: Implement build_command_data payload builder
tasks.description: Pure function in core.rs that converts any RunCommand variant into the logical payload bytes (what goes after the 0x81 0x9F magic header in the wire format). For strings: [string_bytes, 0x03 ETX]. For typed commands: [0xF0, cmd_id, args, 0x03 ETX].

subtasks.title: Implement build_command_data for SendMessage and single-report typed commands
Implement APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT payload in build_command_data
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_b92a9b2b603f/architecture/firmware_wire_contract.md §APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT request layout: [layer][flags][count][id0][id1]…. The layer byte: None => 0xFF (clear), Some(n) => n. The flags byte: bit 0 = clear_board. The count byte: number of callback IDs. Then count callback-id bytes. The firmware caps at HOST_CALLBACK_MAX=32 but the transport is uncapped (PRD §10.1).
2. INPUT: Depends on P1.M2.T1.S1 (build_command_data function exists with a todo!() for ApplyHostContext).
3. LOGIC: Replace the todo!() arm in build_command_data:
   ```rust
   RunCommand::ApplyHostContext { layer, callbacks, clear_board } => {
       let mut data = vec![CMD_DISCRIMINATOR, CMD_APPLY_HOST_CONTEXT];
       data.push(layer.unwrap_or(0xFF));  // None => 0xFF = clear host layer
       let mut flags = 0u8;
       if *clear_board { flags |= 0x01; }  // bit 0 = clear_board
       data.push(flags);
       data.push(callbacks.len().min(255) as u8);  // count, defensive clamp
       data.extend_from_slice(callbacks);           // id0, id1, ...
       data.push(0x03);                              // ETX
       data
   }
   ```
   Add unit tests:
   - layer=None => byte is 0xFF
   - layer=Some(224) => byte is 224
   - clear_board=false => flags byte is 0x00
   - clear_board=true => flags byte is 0x01
   - callbacks=vec![1,5,10] => count=3, followed by [1,5,10]
   - callbacks with >255 elements => count clamped to 255 (edge case)
   - Full byte sequence for a representative ApplyHostContext matches [0xF0,0x05,layer,flags,count,ids...,0x03]
4. OUTPUT: Complete build_command_data handling all RunCommand variants. Consumed by run() dispatch (P1.M3.T3.S1).
5. DOCS: none — internal function.

tasks.title: Implement parse_reply reply parser
tasks.description: Pure function in core.rs that decodes a raw 32-byte device reply buffer into a CommandResponse. Handles typed replies (0x51 marker), legacy match-bool (0/1), and no-reply/timeout.

subtasks.title: Implement parse_reply for typed 0x51 replies
Add legacy/timeout/edge-case handling to parse_reply and finalize tests
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_b92a9b2b603f/architecture/findings_and_risks.md §R2 — the caller treats non-0x51 replies and timeouts as legacy/non-capable devices. The parse_reply function from S1 already handles 0/1 => Legacy. This subtask adds tests for the full edge-case matrix.
2. INPUT: Depends on P1.M2.T2.S1 (parse_reply exists with typed-reply decoding).
3. LOGIC: Verify and add unit tests for ALL edge cases of parse_reply:
   - Empty slice `&[]` => Timeout
   - response[0]==0 => Legacy{matched:false}
   - response[0]==1 => Legacy{matched:true}
   - response[0]==0x51 but response too short (len < 3) => should not panic; defensive .get() returns default values
   - response[0]==0x51, response[1]==0xFF (unknown cmd echo) => Timeout
   - response[0]==0x42 (unknown marker) => Timeout
   - response[0]==0x51, response[1]==0x02, name with non-UTF8 bytes => name should be None (from_utf8 fails => .ok() => None)
   - Truncated Info reply (len=4, missing board_rules_present) => board_rules_present defaults to false
   If any test reveals a bug in S1's implementation, fix it here. This subtask is the safety net.
4. OUTPUT: Fully tested parse_reply covering all wire-protocol reply shapes. Consumed by run() dispatch (P1.M3.T3.S1).
5. DOCS: none — internal function with comprehensive test coverage.

milestones.title: Transport Reply Capture Integration
milestones.description: Evolve the existing transport layer (burst_to_one, try_send_once, send_raw_report) to capture the first device reply instead of discarding all replies, and wire run() to use build_command_data + send_raw_report + parse_reply for full typed-command dispatch. This is the integration milestone that connects the pure functions (M2) to the HID I/O layer.

tasks.title: Evolve burst_to_one to capture first reply
tasks.description: Change burst_to_one from returning bool to returning (bool, Option<Vec<u8>>), where the Option carries the first 32-byte IN-side reply captured with a bounded read_timeout. The existing non-blocking drain of surplus reports is preserved.

subtasks.title: Change burst_to_one return type and add bounded reply capture
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_b92a9b2b603f/architecture/transport_evolution.md §burst_to_one Reply Capture Logic — the exact before/after code. See also external_deps.md §read_timeout semantics — Ok(0) means timeout/no-data (not an error), Ok(n>0) means data read. REPLY_READ_TIMEOUT_MS (1000ms) was added in P1.M1.T2.S1.
2. INPUT: Depends on P1.M1.T2.S1 (REPLY_READ_TIMEOUT_MS constant exists in core.rs). The current burst_to_one is at ~src/core.rs:330, returns bool, has a drain loop using read_timeout(0).
3. LOGIC: In src/core.rs, change burst_to_one:
   a) Change signature: `fn burst_to_one(interface: &HidDevice, data: &[u8], batch_count: usize, verbose: bool) -> (bool, Option<Vec<u8>>)`
   b) Keep the existing write loop unchanged (fill [0x00, 0x81, 0x9F, payload...], write each report). On write error return `(false, None)`.
   c) After all writes succeed, BEFORE the drain loop, add a bounded reply read:
      ```rust
      let mut reply: Option<Vec<u8>> = None;
      let mut read_buf = [0u8; REPORT_LENGTH + 1];
      match interface.read_timeout(&mut read_buf, REPLY_READ_TIMEOUT_MS) {
          Ok(n) if n > 0 => { reply = Some(read_buf[..n].to_vec()); }
          _ => {}  // Ok(0)=timeout, Err=read failure; reply stays None
      }
      ```
   d) Keep the existing drain loop AFTER the reply read (drain surplus with read_timeout(0), bounded by IN_DRAIN_MAX).
   e) Return `(true, reply)`.
   f) Update the call site in try_send_once — this WILL cause a compile error because try_send_once calls `burst_to_one(...)` and expects bool. Add a temporary `.0` access or handle the tuple. The full try_send_once evolution is P1.M3.T2.S1.
   g) Add verbose logging for the captured reply length.
   h) Update the existing burst_to_one doc comment to document the reply capture.
   Unit tests for burst_toone cannot run without hardware — verify via compilation and the fact that the existing drain tests still pass conceptually. Focus on ensuring the function signature change is clean.
4. OUTPUT: burst_to_one returns (bool, Option<Vec<u8>>). The crate may not compile until try_send_once (next subtask) is updated. That is expected — the dependency chain is burst_to_one -> try_send_once -> send_raw_report -> run().
5. DOCS: none — internal function change.

tasks.title: Evolve send_raw_report to propagate reply data
tasks.description: Propagate the captured reply upward through try_send_once and send_raw_report so run() can access it. Changes return types at each level of the call stack.

subtasks.title: Update try_send_once and send_raw_report to propagate first reply

tasks.title: Wire run() full typed-command dispatch
tasks.description: Replace the placeholder/stub logic in run() with the real dispatch: build_command_data -> send_raw_report -> parse_reply -> CommandResponse. This ties together all the work from M1 and M2.

subtasks.title: Implement run() dispatch using build_command_data, send_raw_report, and parse_reply

milestones.title: CLI, Cleanup & Documentation
milestones.description: Add optional diagnostic CLI subcommands, clean up unused dependencies, bump version to 0.3.0, and sync all changeset-level documentation (README.md, cross-doc consistency).

tasks.title: Add diagnostic CLI subcommands
tasks.description: Add optional CLI flags for the typed-command diagnostics: --query-info and --list-callbacks. These map to RunCommand::QueryInfo and a loop of RunCommand::QueryCallback, providing convenient command-line access to the library API.

subtasks.title: Add --query-info and --list-callbacks flags to parse_cli_args
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See PRD §11 — '(Diagnostic subcommands — e.g. query-info, list-callbacks — are optional conveniences over the library API and are not required for qmkonnect, which uses the library directly.)' The current parse_cli_args in src/lib.rs uses clap builder API. The simplest approach is additional flags rather than subcommands (keeps the flat CLI structure).
2. INPUT: Depends on P1.M3.T3.S1 (run() dispatch works so the CLI can exercise typed commands).
3. LOGIC: In src/lib.rs parse_cli_args():
   a) Add a `--query-info` flag (ArgAction::SetTrue). When set, maps to `RunCommand::QueryInfo`.
   b) Add a `--list-callbacks` flag (ArgAction::SetTrue). When set, the CLI runs QueryInfo first to get callback_count, then loops QueryCallback(0..count), printing each CallbackName. This is a CLI-level concern, not a single RunCommand — parse_cli_args returns RunCommand::QueryInfo, and main.rs handles the loop. OR: parse_cli_args returns a special command that main.rs interprets. Keep it simple: --list-callbacks returns RunCommand::QueryInfo and main.rs does the callback sweep if the flag was set.
   c) Update the command selection logic: the priority is --list > --query-info > --list-callbacks > message positional. Each is mutually exclusive with the others (or message).
   d) In main.rs, after run() returns CommandResponse::Info, if --list-callbacks was requested, loop QueryCallback and print results. This requires main.rs to call run() multiple times (once for QueryInfo, then once per callback index).
   e) Note: The PRD says these are OPTIONAL conveniences. If implementation is complex, a minimal --query-info flag that just prints the Info response is sufficient for v0.3.0. --list-callbacks can be deferred.
4. OUTPUT: parse_cli_args supports new flags. main.rs handles the diagnostic output. CLI users can run `qmk_notifier --query-info` to see device capability info.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the CLI usage table in README.md §Command Line Options to include the new flags. Update the --help output description in parse_cli_args.

tasks.title: Dependency cleanup and version bump
tasks.description: Remove unused dependencies (toml, serde, dirs) from Cargo.toml and bump the crate version from 0.2.1 to 0.3.0.

subtasks.title: Remove unused deps and bump version to 0.3.0
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_b92a9b2b603f/architecture/system_context.md §Dependency note — verified by grep that toml, serde, and dirs have ZERO references in src/. Also see external_deps.md §Cargo.toml dependency state. The PRD §2 says 'toml/serde are currently listed but unused after config-file support was removed — they may be dropped.' dirs is also dead code.
2. INPUT: No code dependencies. This is a Cargo.toml-only change.
3. LOGIC: In Cargo.toml:
   a) Change `version = "0.2.1"` to `version = "0.3.0"`.
   b) Remove these three lines from [dependencies]:
      - `toml = "0.8.10"`
      - `serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }`
      - `dirs = "5.0.1"`
   c) Run `cargo build` and `cargo test` to verify nothing breaks. If any test or code references these crates (unexpected), restore the dep and investigate.
   d) Run `cargo update` to refresh Cargo.lock (the lockfile will drop the removed deps and their transitive deps).
   e) Verify `cargo build --release` succeeds.
4. OUTPUT: Leaner Cargo.toml with only clap, hidapi. Version 0.3.0. Cargo.lock updated.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] The Cargo.toml version bump is a config surface change. No separate docs file needed — the README version references are handled by P1.M4.T3.S1.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Final cross-cutting documentation task. Updates README.md and any overview docs to reflect the complete v0.3.0 delta: new typed-command API, CommandResponse return type, CLI subcommands, and version. This task depends on ALL implementing subtasks and runs last.

subtasks.title: Update README.md with v0.3.0 API surface and typed-command documentation
Final cross-document consistency verification
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: PRD §14 lists 8 Key Invariants that must be preserved. This subtask verifies each invariant holds in the final code.
2. INPUT: Depends on P1.M4.T3.S1 (README is updated). All code is final.
3. LOGIC: Perform a final consistency sweep:
   a) Verify PRD §14 Invariant 1: Magic header is 0x81 0x9F; ETX is 0x03. Check core.rs burst_to_one still sets request_data[1]=0x81, request_data[2]=0x9F.
   b) Verify Invariant 2: REPORT_LENGTH=32; hidapi buffer is 33. Check constant unchanged.
   c) Verify Invariant 3: VID/PID None => match any. Check device_matches still uses is_none_or.
   d) Verify Invariant 4: Cache keyed by MatchKey, invalidated on write failure, partial send never retried. Check send_raw_report retry loop unchanged.
   e) Verify Invariant 5: Typed commands reuse same framing + cache. Verify build_command_data output goes through the same send_raw_report path.
   f) Verify Invariant 6: Reply parsing disambiguates 0x51 from 0/1 from timeout. Check parse_reply.
   g) Verify Invariant 7: Crate is transport-only. No window detection, no pattern matching in the codebase.
   h) Verify Invariant 8: Where this SPEC and firmware PRD disagree, firmware wins. Check firmware_wire_contract.md for any discrepancies with the implementation.
   i) Run `cargo test` one final time — all tests pass.
   j) Run `cargo clippy` if available — no warnings.
   Report any drift found. Fix if minor (typos in comments); flag if significant.
4. OUTPUT: Signed-off verification that all 8 key invariants hold in the v0.3.0 codebase.
5. DOCS: [Mode B] This is the final consistency gate of the changeset-level documentation task.
